26th July, 2019 Statutory Instruments 265
IN EXERCISE of the powers contained in section 179 of the
Corporate Insolvency Act, 2017, the following Regulations are
made:

sections.heading: These Regulations may be cited as theCorporate Insolvency Title
sections.text:
1. These Regulations may be cited as theCorporate Insolvency Title
(Insolvency Practitioner Accreditation) Regulations, 2019.

sections.heading: (1) A person who wishes to practice as an insolvency Grant or
sections.text:
2. (1) A person who wishes to practice as an insolvency Grant or
practioner shall apply for accreditation or renewal of accreditation rejection of
application for
as an insolvency practioner in the form set out schedule, on payment accreditation
of the fee set out in the Corporate Insolvency (Form and Fees)
SI. No. of
Regulations, 2019.
2019
(2) The Registrar shall, within foruteen days of receipt of an
application for accreditation—
(a) grant accreditation where the applicant meets the
requirements; or
(b) reject the application where the applicant does not meet
the requirements.
(3) The Registrar shall, where the Registrar rejects an application
in accordance with sub-regulation (2)(b), inform the applicant of
the reasons for rejection.

sections.heading: An accredited insolvency practioner shall— Terms and
sections.text:
3. An accredited insolvency practioner shall— Terms and
conditions
(a) reflect the accreditation number on documents generated attaching to
by that insolvency practioner; and accreditation
number and
(b) display the accreditation certificate at a conspicuous place accreditation
certificate
at the practitioner’s usual place of business.

sections.heading: (1) An insolvency practioner may apply for renewal of Renewal of
sections.text:
4. (1) An insolvency practioner may apply for renewal of Renewal of
accreditation
accreditation with the Registrar three months before the expiry of
the current accreditation.
(2) The Registrar may approve or reject an application for
renewal of accreditation.
(3) The Registrar may reject an application for renewal or
accreditation where the Registrar determines that the requirements
of the Act have not been met.
(4) The Registrar shall furnish the applicant with resons for
rejecting an application for renewal, and where possible, request
the applicant to remedy the cause of the rejection within a specific
period.

sections.heading: The Registra shall publish annually in the Gazette, and on Publication
sections.text:
5. The Registra shall publish annually in the Gazette, and on Publication
of names
the Agency’s website or in a daily newspaper of general circulation, accredited
the names and addresses of accredited insolvency practitioners. insolvency
practioners
